name: Nick Drake
description:
'''Nicholas Rodney Drake''' (19 June 1948 – 25 November 1974) was an English singer-songwriter. An accomplished [[acoustic guitar]]ist, Drake signed to [[Island Records]] at the age of twenty while still a student at [[Fitzwilliam College, Cambridge]]. His debut album, ''[[Five Leaves Left]]'', was released in 1969, and was followed by two more albums, ''[[Bryter Layter]]'' (1971) and ''[[Pink Moon]]'' (1972). 

Drake suffered from [[depression (mood)|depression]] and was reluctant to perform in front of live audiences. Upon completion of ''Pink Moon'', he withdrew from both performance and recording, retreating to his parents' home in rural [[Warwickshire]]. In 1974, at the age of 26, Drake died from an overdose of [[antidepressant]]s, which was ruled a [[suicide]] by the coroner.

Drake did not reach a wide audience during his lifetime, but found posthumous acclaim following the release of ''[[Fruit Tree (box set)|Fruit Tree]]'', a [[box set]] released by his label. He has influenced numerous artists in the [[Folk music|folk]] genre. The first Drake biography in English appeared in 1997; it was followed by the documentary films ''A Stranger Among Us'' in 1999 and ''A Skin Too Few: The Days of Nick Drake'' in 2000.
